title: Aglaia aquea
intro: Aglaia aquea, also known as the water billet, is a species of flowering plant in the mahogany family (Meliaceae). This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly for its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate various health issues, such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Aglaia aquea include lignans, flavonoids, and alkaloids, including aglacones, which have been shown to exhibit significant biological activities. In traditional medicine, Aglaia aquea is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or powder, and is used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections, skin conditions, and fever.
taxonomy: Aglaia aquea is classified within the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany or ginger family. This family is a part of the larger order Sapindales, which includes various other plant families such as Rutaceae and Anacardiaceae. The genus Aglaia, to which Aglaia aquea belongs, is native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The species Aglaia aquea is a type of evergreen tree known for its distinctive leaves and fragrant flowers. Aglaia aquea is often found in moist, subtropical environments, where it provides shade and supports local ecosystems.
uses: Aglaia aquea, also known as the water mango, is a plant species with various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. In some Asian cultures, it is used to treat skin conditions, such as acne and eczema. The plant's properties are also said to have antiseptic and antibacterial effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Aglaia aquea for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Aglaia aquea, also known as the water mangrove, is a plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its extracts have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. Additionally, Aglaia aquea has been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which may help in the prevention of infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and its effects on human health.
properties: Aglaia aquea, a plant native to certain regions of Asia, is reported to have various therapeutic properties. Its leaves and bark are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant contains compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. constituents: Aglaia aquea contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids. These compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits. Flavonoids found in Aglaia aquea may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. parts: Aglaia aquea is a species of plant known for its medicinal properties. preparations: Aglaia aquea is primarily used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations involve the use of its bark, seeds, and leaves. The plant is often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. In herbal preparations, the bark and seeds are typically dried and powdered before being mixed with water or other ingredients to create a tea or decoction.
